item.title: Software Ate My Homework
item.description: A ransomware attack took down a popular university-course-management software right in the middle of finals.
item.dc:creator: Ian Bogost

item.title: The Secret to Understanding AI
item.description: “Imagine the tech without the tech companies.”
item.dc:creator: Josh Tyrangiel

item.title: The Savannah Bananas Bring Back a Negro Leagues Team
item.description: The organization is best known for its dance numbers and trick plays. Now it’s reviving one of the most entertaining—and controversial—franchises in baseball history.
item.dc:creator: Josh Levin

item.title: Trump Shows He’s Still Got Juice
item.description: Yesterday’s election in Indiana sent a message to all Republicans of conscience.
item.dc:creator: David Frum

item.title: A Dangerous New Attack on Press Freedom
item.description: According to MS NOW, the FBI has launched an investigation into anAtlanticreporter.
item.dc:creator: David A. Graham

item.title: The Perverse Tyranny of a Perfect Transcript
item.description: Why Harvard should cap A grades.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Greene

item.title: Progressive Activists Are Sometimes on the Wrong Side of History
item.description: Thinking otherwise can enable the left’s worst instincts, as a speech at the University of Michigan’s commencement showed.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Judicial Supremacy Has Arrived
item.description: Last week’s Supreme Court decision didn’t just undermine the Voting Rights Act. It foreclosed the possibility of any new Voting Rights Act in the future, too.
item.dc:creator: Duncan Hosie

item.title: Why Stocks Keep Going Up
item.description: The boom is not as untethered from reality as it may look.
item.dc:creator: James Surowiecki

item.title: The End of Cigarettes Is Coming
item.description: The U.K. is phasing out smoking. How long will Americans tolerate tobacco—and other vices?
item.dc:creator: Charles Fain Lehman
